title: Kolot
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Character |type= |image= |name=Kolot |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]]''[[X-Wing: Solo Command]]'' |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]]{{Fact}} |pronouns=He/him{{Fact}} |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber=[[Cybernetics/Legends|Prosthetic]] [[Limb/Legends|limb]] extensions |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=[[Gara Petothel]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Kolot, we're the same thing, you and I. We're both lies that eventually became the truth.|Gara Petothel|}} '''Kolot''' was an [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] upon whom [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] [[Zsinj]]'s [[Project Chubar]] experimented. The experiments, which left Kolot able to [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] a [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]], were inspired by the tale of [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Kettch]], a semi-fictional Ewok member of the [[Pirate/Legends|pirating]] [[Hawk-bat Independent Space Force]]. In [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]], Kolot was in the secret [[Laboratory/Legends|laboratories]] aboard the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]] [[Iron Fist (Executor-class)|''Iron Fist'']] when it was in the [[Selaggis system/Legends|Selaggis system]]. There, Kolot and other test subjects were freed by [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]–allied [[Gara Petothel]], and the Ewok helped pilot the escapees to freedom in a [[Sentinel-class landing craft/Legends|''Sentinel''-class landing craft]]. After his escape, the Ewok later became the co-pilot for Petothel, who took the name Kirney Slane, as she headed a shuttling business out of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]]. ==Biography== ===Learning to fly=== {{Quote|Doctors put me in sim-ul-at-ors. See if Kolot can learn to fly.''"
"''And you can.''"
"''Yes.|Kolot and Gara Petothel, on the ''Iron Fist''|}} An [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], Kolot was taken into the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Scientist/Legends|scientific]] program known as [[Project Chubar]] as a test subject. Like others in the program, the Ewok was subjected to tests and other experimentation that increased his intelligence and allowed him to speak the [[Language/Legends|language]] of [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]]; Project Chubar also had the effect of making its test subjects more aggressive and violent.''[[X-Wing: Iron Fist]]'' In [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]],''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' dates the [[Battle of Kuat (Hunt for Zsinj)|Battle of Kuat]], in which [[Zsinj]] learned of [[Kettch]], to [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]]. Imperial [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] [[Zsinj]], who controlled Project Chubar at that [[Time/Legends|time]], learned of a [[Bioengineering/Legends|genetically-modified]] Ewok [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] named [[Kettch]], who supposedly few a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] for the [[Pirate/Legends|pirating]] [[Hawk-bat Independent Space Force]] through the use of [[Cybernetics/Legends|prosthetic]] [[Limb/Legends|limb]] extensions. However, Kettch was merely a semi-fictional prank that [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] pilot [[Wes Janson/Legends|Wes Janson]] had engineered. Inspired by this tale, Zsinj ordered [[Technician/Legends|mechanics]] to create prosthetic limb extensions so Kolot could reach the piloting controls of a [[Starship/Legends|starship]]. Additionally, the [[Doctor/Legends|doctors]] in the program put Kolot through [[Flight simulator/Legends|flight simulators]] to see if the Ewok could learn how to fly. In the end, the experiment was a success, and Kolot was able to fly a [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] despite being an Ewok—a [[Species/Legends|species]] that was known for its general inability to work advanced equipment. ===Rescue at Selaggis=== {{Quote|I'm trying to drum up traffic for my new shuttle business. I have a ship, a ''Sentinel''-class landing shuttle I obtained. I have a copilot you really need to meet and an astromech you already know.|"Kirney Slane" mentions Kolot in a message to [[Myn Donos]], a fellow Wraith|}} [[File:ToppsGS6Kettch.jpg|thumb|right|150px|The push to make Kolot a pilot was inspired by the Ewok Kettch, who was actually a stuffed toy owned by Wraith Squadron.]] In 7 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]],''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' dates the [[Battle of Selaggis]] to [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]]. Kolot was aboard Zsinj's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]] [[Iron Fist (Executor-class)|''Iron Fist'']], in the [[Selaggis system/Legends|Selaggis system]], alongside the rest of Project Chubar, after its base of operations was relocated from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Saffalore]]. At the time that Zsinj's ''Iron Fist'' and a small [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] of ships were [[Battle of Selaggis|attacked]] by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic's]] [[General/Legends|General]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and his [[Solo Fleet|naval group]], [[Gara Petothel]], a New Republic–allied former [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] [[Spy/Legends|spy]], was aboard the ''Iron Fist'' after gaining Zsinj's trust. Responsible for the [[Saboteur/Legends|sabotage]] that left the ''Iron Fist'' with its [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] disabled in the Selaggis [[Star system/Legends|system]], she made her way to the [[Laboratory/Legends|laboratories]] where Kolot and some of the other Project Chubar test subjects were being held. Holding the four [[Medic/Legends|medics]] nearby at [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]]-point, Petothel freed Kolot first from one of the ground-level cages after the Ewok agreed to free the other [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]]. Although Kolot wanted to [[Death/Legends|kill]] the captured medics, Petothel had the Ewok instead go to each cage and, if the inhabitant agreed to not attack Petothel, free them so she could get them off the ''Iron Fist''. When Kolot had released all of the test subjects who had agreed not to attack Petothel, the former Imperial agent asked if any of the test subjects could fly a shuttle so that they could make an escape. Kolot answered that he could, surprising and shocking Petothel, who thought he was joking. She was familiar with Kettch, who actually did not exist, as it was merely a stuffed toy and an alias that had been used for starfighter pilot [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]]. Realizing that the story of Kettch had inspired the creation of Kolot as a pilot, Petothel told a confused Kolot that both he and she were lies that had eventually became true; Petothel herself had infiltrated the New Republic starfighter [[Squadron/Legends|unit]] [[Wraith Squadron]] as an Imperial spy only to later change her allegiance to the Wraiths. Kolot and the others followed Petothel as she led them to safety, though a pair of freed [[Gamorrean/Legends|Gamorreans]] took the time to gore and kill the bound medics, who had inflicted so much pain on the group. Kolot brought with him the limb extensions that had been crafted for him as the group made its way to a [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] bay that had experienced a [[Radiation/Legends|radiation]]-leak alert—which Petothel's [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]], [[Tonin]], had falsified—and cleared the area of Imperial personnel. Petothel and Tonin momentarily went on ahead of the group into the hangar, and there she was confronted by Imperial General [[Melvar]], as well as [[Ensign/Legends|Ensign]] [[Gatterweld]], who had guessed her destination after they had discovered that she had betrayed them. Petothel was saved, however, as Kolot and the group of test subjects overwhelmed the pair. The group tried to kill the Imperials, but Petothel disallowed it, instead having the pair of Zsinj's men bound. While Petothel departed in an [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing starfighter]] that she had brought to the ''Iron Fist'', Kolot flew the rest of the escapees and Tonin free of the [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] in a [[Sentinel-class landing craft/Legends|''Sentinel''-class landing craft]], while the battle raged around them. Although Petothel's [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]] was subsequently shot down on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Selcaron]] in the Selaggis system, she and the rest of the escapees, including Kolot, eventually made their way to the planet [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]]. There, Petothel, who had taken the name Kirney Slane, started a shuttle business, with Kolot as her co-pilot and the ''Sentinel''-class shuttle they had stolen. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|I will kill them.|Kolot, on the bound medics|}} Modified to have an increased intelligence that dwarfed those of other Ewoks, Kolot had many [[Human/Legends|Human]]-like mannerisms, such as his shrug and his nod when he was talking with Petothel. Kolot also had a singsong voice when he spoke in Basic. Due to the involvement that the medics had with him during his time as a test subject, Kolot wished to kill the medics when he was released from his cage, though Petothel prevented him from doing so. The Ewok was also able to use reason to govern his decisions, and when Petothel prevented him for killing the medics and instead tasked him with freeing the other subjects, he complied, as it was his best option. Due to his increased intelligence and the flight simulators that he was put through, Kolot, unlike the majority of his species, was able to pilot a shuttle, albeit with the use of limb extensions. ==Behind the scenes== Kolot first appeared in the novel ''[[X-Wing: Solo Command]]'', written by [[Aaron Allston]] and released in [[1999]] as part of the [[Star Wars: X-Wing (novel series)|''X-Wing'']] novel series. Two of Allston's subsequent novels, ''[[Legacy of the Force: Betrayal]]'' ([[2006]]) and ''[[X-Wing: Mercy Kill]]'' ([[2012]]), suggest that Kolot was still flying as a co-pilot for Petothel''[[Legacy of the Force: Betrayal]]''''[[X-Wing: Mercy Kill]]'' in [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]] and in [[44 ABY]], respectively.''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' According to ''Betrayal'', the co-pilot's seat of one of the shuttles owned by Petothel's company, [[Donoslane Excursions]], was designed for a small, [[Hair/Legends|furry]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]], and ''Mercy Kill'' states that the [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]] of one of Petothel's shuttles smelled like fur. However, as these descriptions do not explicitly correspond to Kolot, this article does not assume that Kolot was still flying as of 44 ABY. ==Appearances== *''[[X-Wing: Solo Command]]'' {{Ab|X-Wing: Solo Command (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1st|in book}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Kolot }} [[Category:Ewoks]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Pilots]]
